steps.title: Enter two years of values
steps.content: At $x=0$, the condominium is worth $186{,}000$. A $3.2\%$ annual increase means the one-year value is $186{,}000(1.032)=191{,}952$.

steps.title: Find the exponential model
steps.content: An exponential model has the form $f(x)=a(b)^x$. Here $a=186{,}000$ and $b=1.032$, so $$f(x)=186{,}000(1.032)^x$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A (‘$f(x)=186,000(0.032)^{x}$’) uses $0.032$ as the whole multiplier, and choice B (‘$f(x)=186,000(0.32)^{x}$’) uses $0.32$; both would shrink the value. Choice D (‘$f(x)=186,000(1.32)^{x}$’) uses $1.32$, a $32\%$ increase. Choice C (‘$f(x)=186,000(1.032)^{x}$’) uses $1.032$, the correct yearly multiplier. C is correct.
